design_philosophy.core_loop: Select Robot Master stage → navigate platforming challenges with pattern-based enemy encounters → defeat Robot Master boss → absorb Master Weapon → repeat until all eight masters defeated → progress to Wily's fortress with escalating challenges
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Pattern memorization vs. execution skill; managing limited weapon energy across stages; optional Beat bird collectible creates incentive to explore beyond minimum path
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become an increasingly powerful robot hero by absorbing defeated enemies' abilities; customize loadout with eight distinct weapons; rescue kidnapped creator from evil brother
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses meaningful mechanical innovation from MM4 (charged Buster upgraded only in area, not fundamentally new); plot twist subverts player expectation (Proto Man isn't villain, Dark Man imposter was) but gameplay remains traditional; introduces Beat collectible system that rewards thorough play without requiring it; maintains series' 'stage select freedom' philosophy while telling linear narrative

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with energy capsules refilled from fallen enemies or scattered pickups
difficulty.checkpointing: None—death restarts stage; no mid-level checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player chooses stage order, can sequence Robot Masters strategically; Master Weapon selection affects subsequent stage difficulty; Beat collectible and Mystery Tank pickups provide power-curve flexibility; Energy Tanks offer persistent health expansion

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon absorption: defeat Robot Master → copy unique Master Weapon with consumable energy; separate weapon energy pool per stage; Mega Buster charge upgraded from MM4; Rush (dog) assists in platforming; Beat (bird) unlocked via eight collectible letters scattered across stages
progression.unlock_mechanism: Robot Masters defeated = weapons unlocked; letter collectibles → Beat unlocked; completing all stages opens Proto Man fortress; defeating four Dark Men stages open final Wily hideout
replayability_hooks: Eight letter collectibles for Beat unlock encourage exploration replay
Stage order freedom creates different weapon availability sequences
Master Weapon puzzle-solving (certain bosses weak to specific weapons) encourages replaying stages with optimal loadout
Speed-running potential via stage sequence optimization

world_building_techniques: Opening kidnapping cutscene establishes conflict (Proto Man seemingly betrays Mega Man)
Stage-based pacing: eight Robot Masters working under Proto Man create escalating tension
Narrative reveal: four Dark Men imposter stages build mystery
Twist ending: real Proto Man saves Mega Man, revealed Dr. Wily orchestrated entire plot as revenge for MM4 events
Character continuity: Dr. Cossack returns from MM4; Kalinka subplot resolved; Beat introduced as gift
Visual storytelling: boss designs (Star Man, Charge Man, Napalm Man) reflect industrial/militaristic theme aligned with Wily's deception

boss_design.philosophy: Eight Robot Masters represent distinct design archetypes (gravity, stone, crystal, etc.); each has unique attack pattern and movement behavior; designed to be solvable via specific Master Weapon counter (weapon triangle implicit in design); four Dark Men act as second-phase difficulty spike with adaptive AI; final Proto Man impostor is skill check before reveal; real Wily fight uses environmental hazard (collapsing ceiling) as mechanic
notable_bosses: Star Man: gravity-shifting mechanics create spatial puzzles during fight
Gyro Man: projectile-based with rotating attack pattern
Stone Man: slow, deliberate movement with area-denial attacks
Charge Man: fast, aggressive pursuit with dash attacks
Napalm Man: missile spam with varied trajectory patterns
Crystal Man: jumping mobility with ice-crystal hazards
Wave Man: water-based attacks with rising/falling difficulty
Dark Man (x4): escalating adaptivity; fourth is Proto Man impostor with high damage output
Proto Man (real): one-hit death escape scene building tension before Wily encounter

faction_indicators: Proto Man's apparent betrayal creates moral ambiguity (was he corrupted or framed?)
Robot Masters serve under Proto Man but lack agency—are they victims or willing accomplices?
Dr. Wily's revenge narrative: justified by MM4 defeat or petty villainy?
Real Proto Man's silent exit suggests outsider status—neither hero nor villain, morally neutral
danger_signals: Robot kidnapping of Dr. Light signals civilization threat
Proto Man leading army of robots = perceived trustworthy ally becomes existential risk
Dr. Wily creating Dark Man specifically to frame Proto Man shows willingness to manipulate truth
Four Dark Men gauntlet represents escalating mechanical threat before final reveal
environmental_storytelling: Wily's fortress design: final stage represents culmination of threat; collapsing ceiling shows structural weakness after repeated defeats
Stage aesthetics reflect Robot Master themes (Charge Man's train interior, Napalm Man's industrial complex, Crystal Man's cavern)
Fortress progression: Dark Men stages are utilitarian/dystopian before Wily's chamber
Proto Man's fortress: cleaner, more ordered than Wily's chaos—visual distinction between rivals
